compiled_state.title: Sprint orchestrator halts after sub-agent review completes without continuing commit workflow
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Bug Description

After a code-reviewer sub-agent returns REVIEW_RESULT: passed, the sprint orchestrator session goes idle and does not automatically proceed to the next step in the commit workflow (record-review.sh → git commit → git push → ticket close → next batch).

## Observed Behavior

The code-reviewer-light sub-agent completed and returned:
```
REVIEW_RESULT: passed
REVIEWER_HASH=db5d5690655b9e0250ec42a17834ce7378d80e516d79a21473c34567fa68989d
MIN_SCORE=5
FINDING_COUNT=0
```

The session then went idle for an extended period. No further steps executed.

## Expected Behavior

After review returns REVIEW_RESULT: passed, the orchestrator should immediately:
1. Run record-review.sh with --reviewer-hash and --expected-hash
2. Commit via git commit
3. Push via git push
4. Close the completed tasks
5. Check for newly unblocked tasks and launch next batch

## Context at Time of Halt

- Sprint: epic f997-ed78 (implementation-plan re-invocation safety)
- Active story: ca76-bb4e
- Batch 4 just completed (tasks b6f7-9f1c and bd1a-14a3 — RED TDD artifacts for REPLAN_ESCALATE)
- Staged files: .test-index, plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/evals/promptfooconfig.yaml, tests/skills/test_implementation_plan_replan_escalate.py
- Diff hash: 9e67d58be25c20c9597fb9f5a5dcf534a0b2a060da7fd5e61b1780e3369b94aa
- test-gate-status: passed
- review: passed (score=5, 0 findings)
- Workflow artifacts dir: /tmp/workflow-plugin-82b3e38fc170aa9d/
- Context usage: This session was a continuation from a prior context-compacted session. The conversation had already accumulated significant context from the previous session (summarized) plus the current session's work. Context pressure was likely high at the point of halt.

## Hypothesis

Context window pressure may have caused the model to stall after receiving the sub-agent result. The orchestrator failed to synthesize the sub-agent's return value and continue executing the next steps. This is consistent with prior incidents where high context load causes the session to not produce further output after a tool result.

## Session State at Halt

- Previous session was compacted and summarized (very long prior context)
- Batch 3 complete: fa7b-d4e9 committed and pushed
- Batch 4 sub-agents complete: b6f7-9f1c (eval) and bd1a-14a3 (pytest RED) both reported success
- Batch 4 formatting fix applied (ruff format)
- Batch 4 record-test-status: passed
- Batch 4 review: passed — HALTED HERE, idle

## Remaining Work at Halt (for resume context)

Tasks still open in ca76-bb4e story:
- 43d6-4603: blocked on bd1a-14a3 and b6f7-9f1c (now should be unblocked after batch 4 closes)

Tasks in 1826-b0ee story (blocked on ca76-bb4e): 0ded-6519, 1104-9112, 6ae1-a527, 6d2e-ffee, d47c-503a
Story a7fa-8c39 (blocked on ca76-bb4e, TRIVIAL, no children)
Tasks in 8350-8f20 story: 615d-27a0, 9d16-96fb

## Reproduction Conditions

- Long session continuing from prior compacted context
- Multiple sub-agent dispatches have already occurred (high cumulative context)
- Review sub-agent returns result
- No user interaction required to continue — orchestrator should self-continue

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral. Ticket references orchestrator behavior (halts after review) AND affected file is skills/sprint/SKILL.md. Dual-signal detected.
comments.author: Main

comments.body: Gate 1a: skipped — CLI_user tag present; intent-aligned assumed

comments.body: Bot-psychologist: Missing CONTEXT ANCHOR after review in sprint. Fix: added CONTEXT ANCHOR after Step 7 explaining review pass is not a stopping point.
comments.author: Main
